response:
```python
import scanpy as sc
import anndata as ad
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import liana as li
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Load the data
adata = ad.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/Data/breast_cancer/breast_cancer.h5ad')

print(f"Dataset shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"Cell types: {adata.obs['celltype'].unique()}")
print(f"Number of cells per type:\n{adata.obs['celltype'].value_counts()}")

# Make variable names unique
adata.var_names_make_unique()

# Prepare data for cell-cell communication analysis
# LIANA expects raw counts, so we'll work with the current data
print("\nPreparing data for cell-cell communication analysis...")

# Set up spatial coordinates in obsm if not already there
if 'spatial' not in adata.obsm.keys():
    adata.obsm['spatial'] = adata.obs[['x', 'y']].values

# Run LIANA for cell-cell communication analysis
print("Running LIANA for cell-cell communication analysis...")

# Use multiple methods for robust results
li.method.singlecellsignalr(adata, groupby='celltype', verbose=True, use_raw=False)
li.method.connectome(adata, groupby='celltype', verbose=True, use_raw=False)
li.method.cellphonedb(adata, groupby='celltype', verbose=True, use_raw=False)

# Get consensus results
consensus = li.multi.df_to_lr(adata, groupby='celltype', 
                             aggregate_method='mean',
                             return_all_lrs=True)

print(f"\nFound {len(consensus)} potential ligand-receptor interactions")

# Filter for significant interactions
significant_interactions = consensus[consensus['magnitude_rank'] < 0.01]  # Top 1% interactions
print(f"Significant interactions (top 1%): {len(significant_interactions)}")

# Analyze communication patterns
print("\nAnalyzing cell-cell communication patterns...")

# Top communicating cell type pairs
top_pairs = significant_interactions.groupby(['source', 'target']).size().reset_index(name='n_interactions')
top_pairs = top_pairs.sort_values('n_interactions', ascending=False)

print("\nTop communicating cell type pairs:")
print(top_pairs.head(10))

# Most active ligand-receptor pairs
top_lrs = significant_interactions.nlargest(20, 'lr_means')[['ligand', 'receptor', 'source', 'target', 'lr_means']]
print("\nTop 20 ligand-receptor interactions:")
print(top_lrs)

# Store results in adata
adata.uns['liana_results'] = {
    'consensus': consensus,
    'significant': significant_interactions,
    'top_pairs': top_pairs,
    'top_lrs': top_lrs
}

# Create summary statistics
communication_summary = {
    'total_interactions': len(consensus),
    'significant_interactions': len(significant_interactions),
    'cell_types_involved': len(set(consensus['source'].unique()) | set(consensus['target'].unique())),
    'most_active_sender': significant_interactions.groupby('source').size().idxmax(),
    'most_active_receiver': significant_interactions.groupby('target').size().idxmax(),
    'top_lr_pair': significant_interactions.iloc[0][['ligand', 'receptor']].tolist() if len(significant_interactions) > 0 else None
}

print(f"\nCommunication Summary:")
print(f"- Total potential interactions: {communication_summary['total_interactions']}")
print(f"- Significant interactions: {communication_summary['significant_interactions']}")
print(f"- Cell types involved in communication: {communication_summary['cell_types_involved']}")
print(f"- Most active sender: {communication_summary['most_active_sender']}")
print(f"- Most active receiver: {communication_summary['most_active_receiver']}")
if communication_summary['top_lr_pair']:
    print(f"- Top ligand-receptor pair: {communication_summary['top_lr_pair'][0]} -> {communication_summary['top_lr_pair'][1]}")

adata.uns['communication_summary'] = communication_summary

# Analyze spatial patterns of communication
print("\nAnalyzing spatial patterns of cell communication...")

# Calculate spatial distances between cell types
from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist, squareform
from scipy.stats import pearsonr

cell_types = adata.obs['celltype'].unique()
spatial_comm_analysis = {}

for ct1 in cell_types:
    for ct2 in cell_types:
        if ct1 != ct2:
            cells1 = adata.obs[adata.obs['celltype'] == ct1]
            cells2 = adata.obs[adata.obs['celltype'] == ct2]
            
            if len(cells1) > 0 and len(cells2) > 0:
                # Get interactions between these cell types
                pair_interactions = significant_interactions[
                    (significant_interactions['source'] == ct1) & 
                    (significant_interactions['target'] == ct2)
                ]
                
                spatial_comm_analysis[f"{ct1}->{ct2}"] = {
                    'n_interactions': len(pair_interactions),
                    'sender_cells': len(cells1),
                    'receiver_cells': len(cells2)
                }

# Store spatial communication analysis
adata.uns['spatial_communication'] = spatial_comm_analysis

print("Spatial communication patterns:")
for pair, info in spatial_comm_analysis.items():
    if info['n_interactions'] > 0:
        print(f"  {pair}: {info['n_interactions']} interactions "
              f"({info['sender_cells']} -> {info['receiver_cells']} cells)")

# Save the results
adata.write('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q13/run_0/output.h5ad')

print(f"\n=== CELL-CELL COMMUNICATION ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===")
print(f"Dataset: {adata.shape[0]} cells, {adata.shape[1]} genes")
print(f"Cell types analyzed: {len(cell_types)}")
print(f"Total potential ligand-receptor interactions: {len(consensus)}")
print(f"Significant interactions (top 1%): {len(significant_interactions)}")
print(f"Results saved to output file with communication patterns and spatial analysis")
```

import scanpy as sc
import anndata as ad
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import liana as li
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Load the data
adata = ad.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/Data/breast_cancer/breast_cancer.h5ad')

print(f"Dataset shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"Cell types: {adata.obs['celltype'].unique()}")
print(f"Number of cells per type:\n{adata.obs['celltype'].value_counts()}")

# Make variable names unique
adata.var_names_make_unique()

# Prepare data for cell-cell communication analysis
# LIANA expects raw counts, so we'll work with the current data
print("\nPreparing data for cell-cell communication analysis...")

# Set up spatial coordinates in obsm if not already there
if 'spatial' not in adata.obsm.keys():
    adata.obsm['spatial'] = adata.obs[['x', 'y']].values

# Run LIANA for cell-cell communication analysis
print("Running LIANA for cell-cell communication analysis...")

# Use multiple methods for robust results
li.method.singlecellsignalr(adata, groupby='celltype', verbose=True, use_raw=False)
li.method.connectome(adata, groupby='celltype', verbose=True, use_raw=False)
li.method.cellphonedb(adata, groupby='celltype', verbose=True, use_raw=False)

# Get consensus results
consensus = li.multi.df_to_lr(adata, groupby='celltype', 
                             aggregate_method='mean',
                             return_all_lrs=True)

print(f"\nFound {len(consensus)} potential ligand-receptor interactions")

# Filter for significant interactions
significant_interactions = consensus[consensus['magnitude_rank'] < 0.01]  # Top 1% interactions
print(f"Significant interactions (top 1%): {len(significant_interactions)}")

# Analyze communication patterns
print("\nAnalyzing cell-cell communication patterns...")

# Top communicating cell type pairs
top_pairs = significant_interactions.groupby(['source', 'target']).size().reset_index(name='n_interactions')
top_pairs = top_pairs.sort_values('n_interactions', ascending=False)

print("\nTop communicating cell type pairs:")
print(top_pairs.head(10))

# Most active ligand-receptor pairs
top_lrs = significant_interactions.nlargest(20, 'lr_means')[['ligand', 'receptor', 'source', 'target', 'lr_means']]
print("\nTop 20 ligand-receptor interactions:")
print(top_lrs)

# Store results in adata
adata.uns['liana_results'] = {
    'consensus': consensus,
    'significant': significant_interactions,
    'top_pairs': top_pairs,
    'top_lrs': top_lrs
}

# Create summary statistics
communication_summary = {
    'total_interactions': len(consensus),
    'significant_interactions': len(significant_interactions),
    'cell_types_involved': len(set(consensus['source'].unique()) | set(consensus['target'].unique())),
    'most_active_sender': significant_interactions.groupby('source').size().idxmax(),
    'most_active_receiver': significant_interactions.groupby('target').size().idxmax(),
    'top_lr_pair': significant_interactions.iloc[0][['ligand', 'receptor']].tolist() if len(significant_interactions) > 0 else None
}

print(f"\nCommunication Summary:")
print(f"- Total potential interactions: {communication_summary['total_interactions']}")
print(f"- Significant interactions: {communication_summary['significant_interactions']}")
print(f"- Cell types involved in communication: {communication_summary['cell_types_involved']}")
print(f"- Most active sender: {communication_summary['most_active_sender']}")
print(f"- Most active receiver: {communication_summary['most_active_receiver']}")
if communication_summary['top_lr_pair']:
    print(f"- Top ligand-receptor pair: {communication_summary['top_lr_pair'][0]} -> {communication_summary['top_lr_pair'][1]}")

adata.uns['communication_summary'] = communication_summary

# Analyze spatial patterns of communication
print("\nAnalyzing spatial patterns of cell communication...")

# Calculate spatial distances between cell types
from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist, squareform
from scipy.stats import pearsonr

cell_types = adata.obs['celltype'].unique()
spatial_comm_analysis = {}

for ct1 in cell_types:
    for ct2 in cell_types:
        if ct1 != ct2:
            cells1 = adata.obs[adata.obs['celltype'] == ct1]
            cells2 = adata.obs[adata.obs['celltype'] == ct2]
            
            if len(cells1) > 0 and len(cells2) > 0:
                # Get interactions between these cell types
                pair_interactions = significant_interactions[
                    (significant_interactions['source'] == ct1) & 
                    (significant_interactions['target'] == ct2)
                ]
                
                spatial_comm_analysis[f"{ct1}->{ct2}"] = {
                    'n_interactions': len(pair_interactions),
                    'sender_cells': len(cells1),
                    'receiver_cells': len(cells2)
                }

# Store spatial communication analysis
adata.uns['spatial_communication'] = spatial_comm_analysis

print("Spatial communication patterns:")
for pair, info in spatial_comm_analysis.items():
    if info['n_interactions'] > 0:
        print(f"  {pair}: {info['n_interactions']} interactions "
              f"({info['sender_cells']} -> {info['receiver_cells']} cells)")

# Save the results
adata.write('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/A_vanilla/Q13/run_0/output.h5ad')

print(f"\n=== CELL-CELL COMMUNICATION ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===")
print(f"Dataset: {adata.shape[0]} cells, {adata.shape[1]} genes")
print(f"Cell types analyzed: {len(cell_types)}")
print(f"Total potential ligand-receptor interactions: {len(consensus)}")
print(f"Significant interactions (top 1%): {len(significant_interactions)}")
print(f"Results saved to output file with communication patterns and spatial analysis")
